title: Mediterranean Eggplant
subtitle: with Cubanelle Chermoula and Apricot Couscous
description: Chermoula is a marinade hailing from Northern Africa. It has a smoky, citrus-y taste. Here, we use it to complement the roasted texture of the eggplant and the sweetness of apricot couscous.

steps.title: PREP THE VEGETABLES
steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Cut the eggplant into rounds on the diagonal. Deseed the cubanelle pepper and roughly chop.

steps.title: ROAST THE EGGPLANT
steps.text: Add ¾ cup water to a small saucepan and bring to a boil. Add the whole wheat couscous, cover, and remove from heat. Let couscous stand until Step 5. Transfer eggplant to baking sheet and coat with 4 tbsp olive oil. Season generously with salt and pepper and place in the oven. Roast until eggplant is browned on the bottom and soft, about 30 to 35 minutes.

steps.title: CHAR THE PEPPER
steps.text: Place a medium skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tsp vegetable oil. Once hot, add the chopped cubanelle to the skillet. Cook, turning occasionally, until charred and softened, about 5 to 7 minutes. Remove from the heat to cool.

steps.title: MAKE THE CHERMOULA
steps.text: Peel and grate the ginger. Grate 1 clove garlic into a medium bowl and add the ginger. Chop the dill fronds and add to the bowl. Zest the lemon and add just 1 tsp lemon zest to the bowl. Add the smoked paprika, cumin, ¼ tsp salt, 2 tbsp olive oil, and the juice from half the lemon. Finely chop the charred cubanelle pepper, add to the bowl and stir to combine.

steps.title: FINISH THE COUSCOUS
steps.text: Finely chop the dried apricots. Fluff the cooked couscous with a fork and add the apricots, currants, lima beans, remaining lemon zest and juice, and a pinch of salt. Stir to combine.

steps.title: SERVE
steps.text: Spread the vegan yogurt on the bottom of large plates. Top with apricot couscous, roasted eggplant, and cubanelle chermoula. Enjoy!
